title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Jiles leaves the room, Molly Davis questions Chief Trotter regarding the Lorrach Farm case. Molly challenges the assumption that the eldest son, Christopher, is the killer, suggesting the possibility of other relatives, such as the father, who was serving overseas in the military. Trotter notes that while the boy has an unstable mental state and is a military deserter, he has considered all possibilities, including the possibility of a woman being the killer. Trotter expresses a belief that the killer is someone who enjoys their actions.

Molly retreats to the kitchen to find comfort in domestic chores. Christopher Lane enters, reporting that someone has stolen a police officer's skis from a cabinet under the stairs, an event that has made Trotter uneasy. Christopher expresses excitement about the situation, which causes Molly to shudder as she recalls the purple face of the deceased Mrs. Poole.

Molly asks Christopher to stay because she is afraid of being alone. Christopher observes that Molly is not afraid of him, despite his admission that he is the only person with a motive and a profile fitting the killer. Molly maintains her belief in other possibilities. During their conversation, Christopher admits that "Christopher Lane" is an alias and reveals that he is actually a deserter from the army. He explains that his desertion was driven by a mental breakdown following the death of his mother in an air raid, which led to a compulsion to dig her up. Molly attempts to comfort him, suggesting that he can start over.
